flashcards.definition: The point where the graph crosses the x-axis; found by setting y = 0.
The point where the graph crosses the y-axis; found by evaluating f(0).
f(−x) = f(x); the graph is symmetric about the y-axis.
f(−x) = −f(x); the graph is symmetric about the origin.
f(a) < f(b) whenever a < b on an interval; the graph rises from left to right.
